source_family_role: stretchable bases: panels, button states, bars, frames, and chips without unique center decoration in slice9 stretch zones
prompt:
Create a production source sheet for runtime-ui-map-kit. Source family: blank UI kit sheet. Role: stretchable bases: panels, button states, bars, frames, and chips without unique center decoration in slice9 stretch zones. This is a cuttable source sheet, not a gameplay screenshot, mockup, landing page, or composed UI screen. Use a perfectly flat chroma background #00ff00 or true transparency; do not use gradients, shadows, glow, or texture in the background. Keep every asset isolated with generous gutters and no overlap between shadows. Keep all labels, numbers, quest text, counters, and state values blank for runtime composition. For slice9 bases, keep stretch zones structurally boring: corners, straight edges, fill, and repeatable texture only. Put unique gems, badges, medallions, locks, banners, and center ornaments in separate overlay sprites, not inside stretchable bases. Use consistent perspective, lighting, material language, and line weight across the sheet.

Compact-v3 focus: include a separate compact/mobile button family designed for 128x48, 180x56, and 240x64 runtime targets, plus compact reward chips/status strips. These controls must stay visually rich through material, bevel, edge texture, and separate overlay accents, not through oversized corner caps or center ornaments baked into the stretchable base. Corners must fit inside small controls without being cropped; long horizontal centers must remain readable when stretched; vertical margins must be small enough for 48-64px high buttons. Also include one narrow compact journal/objective panel for portrait use, with a plain center and no top ornament that collides with runtime text.
negative_prompt: button labels, resource counters, timer values, tutorial text, debug text, game state values, quest state, map labels, random letters, watermarks, labels fused into reusable backgrounds, icons fused into buttons, wrong subject, weak silhouette at gameplay size, opaque chroma-key background, baked board state, busy label overlaps, debug-looking button grid, fake-shot crop artifacts, slice9 edge ornament stretches visibly, magenta fringe after chroma removal, readable text, fake letters, watermark, fused icons inside buttons, cropped silhouettes, merged components, busy stretch centers, non-flat chroma background, purple or red-blue halo on transparent edges
constraints: no fused icons inside button backgrounds
transparent or flat chroma-key background for isolated assets
no unique decoration inside slice9 stretch zones

relevant_asset_groups.need: One primary action button, small secondary buttons, journal panel, reward chip, status strips, and selected/disabled/affordable states.
relevant_asset_groups.content_policy: runtime labels/icons stay inside recorded content safe area
relevant_asset_groups.stretch_zone_policy: center and long edges must be plain or repeatable texture; unique medallions, gems, badges, caps, and center ornaments cannot live in stretch zones
relevant_asset_groups.decor_overlay_policy: non-stretch ornaments must be corner-only or exported as separate ui decor overlay sheet sprites with runtime anchors

relevant_asset_groups.need: Combat-only action buttons and enemy panel; do not keep combat grid visible during exploration.
relevant_asset_groups.content_policy: runtime combat labels/icons stay inside recorded content safe area
relevant_asset_groups.stretch_zone_policy: combat panels and buttons use the same plain/repeatable stretch zones; encounter-specific ornamentation is composed as overlays
relevant_asset_groups.decor_overlay_policy: enemy badges, danger caps, locks, and combat flourishes must be separate anchored sprites, not baked into resizable bases

relevant_asset_groups.need: Separate non-stretch corner caps, gems, badges, medallions, lock plates, ribbon banners, dividers, and small fantasy flourishes with transparent/chroma padding.
relevant_asset_groups.anchor_policy: record center or corner anchor before runtime composition; never bake these overlays into slice9 stretch zones

final_asset_policy.notes: Current procedural slice9 bases are a temporary geometry/proof scaffold after the lead rejected sliced generated panels. Final UI art must replace those bases with generated or artist-authored layered source families.

acceptance_checklist: Source sheet is not a full gameplay screenshot.
Background is flat #00ff00 or true transparent alpha.
No readable text, fake glyphs, labels, numbers, or watermark.
Components have clear gutters and do not share antialias/shadow pixels.
Slice9 centers and long edges have no unique ornaments that will stretch.
Ornaments that should not stretch are separate overlay sprites with visible isolation.
Compact buttons preview cleanly at 128x48, 180x56, and 240x64 without clipped corners or squeezed bevels.
Compact/portrait panel preview has a clear title/content safe area and does not require large top decoration.
Icons/sprites have full silhouettes with padding for alpha trim.
Source can pass source-sheet intake before crop rectangles are trusted.
Accepted output will get a generation record with provider/model/workflow/seed or no-seed reason.
